issue_title: Cloudinary upload provider cannot upload files larger than 100 MB
issue_body:
## Bug report

### Required System information
- Strapi version: 4.9.0

### Describe the bug

When uploading any file larger than 100 MB using the [Cloudinary provider](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@strapi/provider-upload-cloudinary) the file is rejected with a HTTP 413.
 
### Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Setup Strapi with the Cloudinary upload provider.
2. Upload a file larger than 100 MB.
3. The upload fails.


### Expected behavior
The file is uploaded just fine.

### Additional context

This happens because Cloudinary limits the file size for the regular upload method to 100 MB. For larger files, they require [chunking the file](https://cloudinary.com/documentation/upload_images#chunked_asset_upload). (See also https://support.cloudinary.com/hc/en-us/community/posts/360009586100/comments/360002140099)
pr_body:
Cloudinary limits the filesize to 100 MB when uploading in a single request: https://cloudinary.com/documentation/upload_images#chunked_asset_upload

Larger files must be uploaded in chunks.

Use the `upload_chunked_stream` to upload all files in chunks. This works for small files just as well.

Fix #16601
